text: Quiet morning across the TX/OK Panhandles. Lows in the upper 40s, light winds. By midweek a sharp Pacific front pushes through with strong NW winds, falling pressure, and cooler temps. Highs drop 20+ degrees Tuesday night into Wednesday.

Drought monitor update: most of the central and eastern Panhandle remains in D2 (Severe). Wheeler, Donley, Hall, and Briscoe counties trending toward D3 (Extreme) without meaningful rain in the next 10 days.
First freeze probability for the central Panhandle: 30% by October 16, 60% by October 22, 85% by November 1. Below normal precipitation continues to dominate the eight-to-fourteen-day outlook.

Watch our forecast for next Wednesday Oct 14: barometric pressure drops 0.35-0.45 inHg through the morning ahead of the front. Wind shift to NW 25 gusting 40. Cooler temps follow with overnight lows in the upper 30s by Thursday.
Burn ban remains in effect for Wheeler, Donley, Collingsworth, and Hall counties. Fine fuels at critical levels. Please be cautious with welding and grinding outdoors.
Crisp morning Friday. Lows 41-46 across the Panhandle. Quiet weekend ahead with seasonal highs in the lower 70s. Big change midweek next week with a cold front.
Monthly rainfall departure for September across the central Panhandle: -1.84 inches. Pasture conditions continue to deteriorate.
